bead.title: Author docs/install/claude-code.md — drop-in install for Claude Code
bead.description:
PRD R-7 ships HELIX as a Claude Code skill. There is no install doc today. Author docs/install/claude-code.md covering:

1. Two install paths:
   a) User-global: copy skills/helix/ to ~/.claude/skills/helix/ (or symlink); workflows/ + docs/helix/ to a discoverable location
   b) Per-repo: vendor skills/helix/ into .claude/skills/helix/ and check workflows/ into the repo
2. How invocation works: the user types /helix or asks for align/frame/evolve etc.; Claude Code resolves the skill by name; the skill's routing table picks the workflow mode.
3. The artifact catalog: where workflows/phases/* lives relative to the install; how the skill resolves template/prompt/meta paths.
4. Minimum Claude Code version + any frontmatter caveats.
5. A 'verify' snippet: a small interaction that confirms the skill is loaded and the catalog reachable.
6. Pointer to the per-runtime contract: read/write/search files only.

Length target: ~150-250 lines. No HELIX CLI commands. Examples use natural-language prompts the user types into Claude Code, not shell commands.
bead.acceptance: docs/install/claude-code.md exists and: names both global and per-repo install paths; explains skill discovery + routing; explains where workflows/ lives and how the skill finds it; gives a verification interaction; contains zero shell invocations of 'helix' or 'ddx' as required surface (DDx is allowed as one optional integration). The doc is reachable from the install README index.
